mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 3.0% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.5% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS < 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and income growth, which sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 132,843 thousand reflects ongoing employment levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth (Spearman rho < 0.15, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts below 1,800 thousand units signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future construction activity, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 8% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is positive (rho > 0.10, p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises above 5.5%, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market softening. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as stagflation concerns emerge.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO deceleration and UNRATE rise shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand and discretionary spending strength. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth (Spearman rho < 0.12, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 195.0 index points while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, creating a negative real interest rate environment. This interaction signals monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations contract.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.4pp over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 62% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE increases and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is positive (rho > 0.12, p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline month-over-month, signaling demand-side weakness. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risks materialize.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS declines MoM
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 10,993.3 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 352,138 million dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 56% of triggered instances, or the joint effect shows Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.
